artists.name: Maya Chen
artists.shortDescription: Award-winning artist known for intricate fine-line botanicals and delicate geometric patterns. Her work blends East Asian calligraphy influences with modern minimalism.

artists.name: Jake Rivera
artists.shortDescription: Old-school meets new-school. Jake brings bold traditional Americana to life with vibrant saturated colors and thick confident lines. Featured in Inked Magazine.

artists.name: Luna Park
artists.shortDescription: Specializing in large-scale Japanese irezumi and photorealistic black & grey portraits. Every piece is custom-designed through close client collaboration.

artists.name: Riko Tanaka
artists.shortDescription: Geometric and dotwork specialist from Osaka. Riko creates hypnotic sacred geometry pieces, mandalas, and ornamental designs using precision hand-poke and machine techniques.

artists.name: Nina Sari
artists.shortDescription: Watercolor and illustrative tattoo artist based in Bali. Nina's signature style combines soft washes of color with bold linework, creating pieces that look like paintings on skin.

artists.name: Dimas Prasetyo
artists.shortDescription: Blackwork and tribal fusion artist. Dimas draws from Polynesian, Dayak, and Mentawai tribal traditions to create bold contemporary pieces rooted in Indonesian heritage.

artists.name: Arief Gunawan
artists.shortDescription: Photorealistic portrait specialist with a background in fine arts. Arief transforms photographs into breathtaking skin art, from celebrity likenesses to beloved family members and pets.

artists.name: Karin Wolff
artists.shortDescription: Berlin-trained artist who merges European illustration with Southeast Asian motifs. Known for intricate floral sleeves, whimsical animal pieces, and bold color palettes that pop.

artists.name: Hendra Kusuma
artists.shortDescription: Lettering and script master. From elegant calligraphy to street-style graffiti lettering, Hendra crafts custom typography tattoos that tell stories through words and flourishes.

artists.name: Siska Maharani
artists.shortDescription: Trash Polka and abstract expressionist. Siska combines chaotic brushstrokes, newspaper clippings, and photorealism into raw, emotional compositions that defy convention.
artists.speciality: Trash Polka, Abstract, Surrealism, Mixed Media

studios.name: Ink Haven Studio
studios.address: Jl. Kemang Raya No. 45, Jakarta Selatan

reviews.comment: Maya is incredibly talented! The fine-line work is so precise and beautiful. She took the time to understand exactly what I wanted and the result exceeded my expectations.
Absolutely stunning work. Maya has such a steady hand and the level of detail is remarkable.
Beautiful work, very delicate and exactly the style I was looking for. The end result is perfect.
Jake is the GOAT of traditional tattooing. Bold lines, perfect color saturation. The panther looks like it's about to jump off my skin!
Really solid work. Jake knows his craft and the colors are vibrant.
Incredible experience from start to finish. Jake's design was even better than what I had in mind.
Luna's Japanese work is on another level. The flow of the koi with the water and waves is seamless.
The realism is amazing — it looks exactly like my dog! Luna captured every little detail.
Riko's precision is mind-blowing. Every line is perfectly straight, every dot perfectly placed.
The mandala is absolutely perfect. Riko spent hours getting every single dot just right.
Nina's watercolor technique is beautiful. The colors blend so naturally.
I'm in love with this tattoo! Nina perfectly captured my cat's personality with the illustrative style.
Dimas is a master of tribal work. He incorporated both Polynesian and Dayak elements seamlessly.
Dimas transformed my terrible old tattoo into something incredible. His cover-up skills are legendary.
Massive piece that took multiple sessions. Dimas was patient and the final result is powerful.
Arief captured my grandmother's smile perfectly. The detail in the eyes is extraordinary. I cried when I saw the result. A true master of realism.
Arief turned my Salvador Dali inspiration into something uniquely mine. The depth and shading are insane.
The lion looks incredibly lifelike. Arief's shading technique creates amazing depth. Solid work.
Karin's use of color is unlike anything I've seen. The wolf looks majestic with the floral elements. Absolutely breathtaking!
Karin designed the most beautiful sleeve I could imagine. Every angle reveals new details. Worth flying to Bali for!
The colors are vibrant and the design wraps my wrist perfectly. Karin has a great eye for placement.
Hendra's lettering is flawless. The Arabic script flows beautifully and the line weight variation is masterful.
The Chicano style lettering is authentic and clean. Hendra added roses and filigree that tied it all together. True artistry.
Bold lettering that reads perfectly from every angle. Hendra took time to adjust sizing and spacing. Great results.
Siska's Trash Polka is raw and emotional. The contrast between the realistic heart and the abstract splatter is powerful.
The raven looks both beautiful and chaotic — exactly what I wanted. Siska has a unique vision that stands out.
Unlike any tattoo I've ever seen. The newspaper texture and red accents make it look like a living collage on my arm.
